solutions.title: Tutorial
solutions.content:
1533B - Nearest Point Function
Of course, we can't choose such $$$y$$$ that $$$y  \lt  x_1$$$, $$$y  \gt  x_n$$$ or $$$y = x_i$$$ for some $$$i$$$, because there will be exactly one nearest point in the array $$$x$$$. So, we need to choose such $$$y$$$ that $$$x_i  \lt  y  \lt  x_{i + 1}$$$ for some $$$i$$$ from $$$1$$$ to $$$n-1$$$. To make both points $$$x_i$$$ and $$$x_{i + 1}$$$ nearest to the point $$$y$$$, we need to choose $$$y = \frac{x_i + x_{i + 1}}{2}$$$. But this is not always possible, because if $$$x_i$$$ and $$$x_{i + 1}$$$ have different parities, the value $$$y$$$ will be non-integer. So, all we have to do in this problem is to check if there is some pair $$$x_i$$$ and $$$x_{i + 1}$$$ that they have the same parity. If there is such a pair, we can break the function, otherwise we can't. Choosing non-adjacent values in $$$x$$$ is wrong, though, because if we choose such $$$x_i$$$ and $$$x_j$$$ that $$$|j - i|  \gt  1$$$ then there will be other points from $$$x$$$ that are nearest to the point $$$y = \frac{x_i + x_j}{2}$$$ (i.e. either $$$x_i$$$ or $$$x_j$$$ will not be the nearest point to $$$y$$$ in such a case).

solutions.title: Solution (vovuh)
solutions.content:
fun main
()
{
val t
=
readLine
()!!.
toInt
()
repeat
(
t
)
{
val n
=
readLine
()!!.
toInt
()
val x
=
readLine
()!!.
split
(
" "
).
map
{
it
.
toInt
()
}
var
ans
=
false
for
(
i
in
1
until
n
)
{
if
((
x
[
i
]
-
x
[
i
-
1
])
%
2
==
0
)
{
ans
=
true
}
}
println
(
if
(
ans
)
"YES"
else
"NO"
)
}
}
